Chef is in the UK shortage occupation list - which means it is easier for employers to get staff from overseas. However, the minimum wage requirement is set at £29,570 per year (or nearly £570 per week gross). I think you better ask the relative in the UK first whether he/she is willing to employ a member of staff on the books for £570 per week.

經過多番考慮後，我終於搵到人生第一份長工正職，做廚房，返左兩個禮拜啦，希望第時可以技術移民到加拿大

比返background先：我上年polyU工程grad，grad左之後去左加拿大做義工8個月，5月回港，計劃想移民去加拿大。屋企唔使我賺錢，但亦唔會供養我。

可能一般人會覺得我U grad走去做廚房傻的嗎。我自己其實無乜感覺，都係一份工啫。但問題係呢個社會依然有人會覺得做廚房係低下工作（至少我既朋友圈子係咁，所以仲未同朋友講我做緊廚房）。但咁既選擇係有原因既。

返到香港第一件事就係立即搵工。同我同期畢業既，做返本行工程，起薪點過到10k都算好彩，而且一般都要求返大陸跟廠做野。做其他行業仲好少少。所以我一開始搵工都搵其他行業文職（唔考慮保險地產銀行），例如採購／hr／秘書都見過工。而我另一方面都計過，如果要係3-4年內儲到一筆錢移民，大概30-40萬，咁我起薪點至少要13k。咁我隨住呢個目標搵工，cv發左過百份，見左十幾次工，雖然我唔急，有D hea住搵，但反反覆覆過左兩個月都仲未有起色。

期間我都一路搜集緊移民加拿大既門路，結論主要都係要係當地搵到工先會最容易加分。而技術移民的話，係香港工作經驗至少要有2-3年。如果我係香港做一份普通文職，經驗會唔會count in都不在話下，用呢份普通文職既經驗係加拿大搵工將會更加渺茫。實情係，除非我有一D專業資格（e.g.醫生護士，有牌照建築師etc.），如非的話移民過去好難搵返本行，通常只會做返低技術工種。既然係咁我搵文職都係無意思，所以我把心一橫直接學廚（其他低技術工種有好多人講過既水電煤，但我係女仔 化妝髮型按摩都可以，但我無興趣）（btw低技術不等如低收入，藍領收入其實部分行業已經高過白領好多）

之後我轉做搵廚房既工，但亦都有條件，唔做連鎖、小食、快餐、小店。總之我要一份寫得上cv亦學到野既，起薪點一樣至少要13k。哈哈然後我見既第一份工就請左我。

btw如果第時儲錢順利既話，想考慮去ottawa讀藍帶，感覺讀完藍帶再當地搵工會更上一層樓。有無人知道讀藍帶個D課程會唔會對移民有幫助？完成課程後會唔會批一兩年我搵工？

p.s. 我想嫁去加拿大 巴打娶我過去啦

Requirements for indefinite leave to enter the United Kingdom as a foreign or Commonwealth citizen discharged from HM Forces
276L. The requirements for indefinite leave to enter the United Kingdom as a foreign or Commonwealth citizen discharged from HM Forces are that the applicant:
(i) has completed at least four years’ service with HM Forces; and
(ii) was discharged from HM Forces on completion of engagement; and
(iii) was not discharged from HM Forces more than 2 years prior to the date on which the application is made; and
(iv) holds a valid United Kingdom entry clearance for entry in this capacity; and
(v) does not fall for refusal under the general grounds for refusal.

可能一般人會覺得我U grad走去做廚房傻的嗎。我自己其實無乜感覺，都係一份工啫。但問題係呢個社會依然有人會覺得做廚房係低下工作（至少我既朋友圈子係咁，所以仲未同朋友講我做緊廚房）。但咁既選擇係有原因既。

返到香港第一件事就係立即搵工。同我同期畢業既，做返本行工程，起薪點過到10k都算好彩，而且一般都要求返大陸跟廠做野。做其他行業仲好少少。所以我一開始搵工都搵其他行業文職（唔考慮保險地產銀行），例如採購／hr／秘書都見過工。而我另一方面都計過，如果要係3-4年內儲到一筆錢移民，大概30-40萬，咁我起薪點至少要13k。咁我隨住呢個目標搵工，cv發左過百份，見左十幾次工，雖然我唔急，有D hea住搵，但反反覆覆過左兩個月都仲未有起色。

期間我都一路搜集緊移民加拿大既門路，結論主要都係要係當地搵到工先會最容易加分。而技術移民的話，係香港工作經驗至少要有2-3年。如果我係香港做一份普通文職，經驗會唔會count in都不在話下，用呢份普通文職既經驗係加拿大搵工將會更加渺茫。實情係，除非我有一D專業資格（e.g.醫生護士，有牌照建築師etc.），如非的話移民過去好難搵返本行，通常只會做返低技術工種。既然係咁我搵文職都係無意思，所以我把心一橫直接學廚（其他低技術工種有好多人講過既水電煤，但我係女仔 化妝髮型按摩都可以，但我無興趣）（btw低技術不等如低收入，藍領收入其實部分行業已經高過白領好多）

之後我轉做搵廚房既工，但亦都有條件，唔做連鎖、小食、快餐、小店。總之我要一份寫得上cv亦學到野既，起薪點一樣至少要13k。哈哈然後我見既第一份工就請左我。

btw如果第時儲錢順利既話，想考慮去ottawa讀藍帶，感覺讀完藍帶再當地搵工會更上一層樓。有無人知道讀藍帶個D課程會唔會對移民有幫助？完成課程後會唔會批一兩年我搵工？

p.s. 我想嫁去加拿大 巴打娶我過去啦

https://www.facebook.com/HongkongerinUK/posts/983529821747177:0

2015年11月某一天，歷時8個月的Tier 1 Entrepreneur Visa 申請终於成功，心情難免激動，但我清楚知道，由這一刻開始，離開香港的時間已在倒數
我記得成功後也沒説給太多人知道，工作亦如常，甚至加倍努力，心裡總是希望多做一點，當然亦有可能是用工作去逃避終於要離開香港的事實
用了差不多一個月時間沉澱，想了很多很多，就像投影機一幕幕的回想在香港成長的日子，有時又貪婪的翻起舊相簿來，就是不捨吧，這點我懂。好友直接問我：有想過放棄嗎？我猶豫了一秒回答：就這麼一剎那吧。縱使不捨，也不回頭了
到了十二月初知道不能再拖了，但香港仍有太多事情要處理，正式移居日子未能落實，惟有先決定16年1月landing，先取BRP, 並在暫定的落腳地Manchester 作較深入的考察，待回港後再決定移居的日子。以前也到過Manchester 兩三次，但旅遊/考察/居住 是完全兩碼子的事，這點我現在很清楚
2016年1月，當我離開香港時，雖然知道只是停留英國十多天，依然百感交集，因爲我明白自己的角色/身分/未來也隨著改變，十二小時的機程也沒睡著，所以下機時倦得可以
拿著 BNO 入境時，關員只是問了數條我幾乎已預計好的問題，當他在我的 Entrepreneur Visa 蓋上印章時，我還是舒了口氣 （因為這不是必然的），我禮貌的說 Thank you , 他的回應竟是「唔該」，我張著口不懂反應，我想我頭髮亂亂愕然的表情很是有趣，他笑著友善的説：我曾到過香港呢。如是這般，我正式以企業家移民簽證入境，第一年居英的日子計算，也正式開始了
在這一年前，我還在考慮移民英國的可能性，心情忐忑得七上八落。一年後，我在這了
只要心還未老 夢想從不怕晚

In very simple terms, if you were born before the 1 January 1983, you can only get British Citizenship from your father only. On the other hand, on or after 1 January 1983, you can get British Citizenship via either your father or mother.

As long as your father (or mother if you were born on or after 1 January 1983) had British Citizenship and married to your mother when you were born in Hong Kong, then you are a British Citizen by descent. This means that you are entitle to a British Citizen passport. You can apply using the link below -

https://www.gov.uk/overseas-passports

As a British Citizen by descent, you cannot automatically pass on your British Citizenship to your children born outside of the UK.

Your sister, on the other hand, is a British Citizen otherwise than by descent (as she was born in the UK). This means that she can pass on her British Citizenship to her children born outside of the UK.

However, if your parents were not married at the time of your birth in Hong Kong or only your mother held British Citizenship and you were born before 1 January 1983, you will need to apply for registration as British Citizen. Please see link below for details -

https://www.gov.uk/register-british-citizen/born-before-1983-to-british-mother

https://www.gov.uk/register-british-citizen/born-before-2006-british-father

Disclaimer: I live in Canada and I am not too familiar with US situations, just know a little bit

If you really admire America as a country, by all means & best of luck. If you are only looking to leave the grip of the Chinese regime, there are more countries than USA. And just because a country accept you as immigrant, doesn't mean you can survive. Here in Canada, it's rather common for many middle-eastern and Indian professional immigrants with PhD or 10+ years work ended up driving taxi or being tech support in call centers, or simply had to go home. Those got it going well for them had made tremendous hard work. It's not all sunny way.

The uncertainty of immigration policy makes me weary about using education as path to immigration. By the time you finish your degree, the same immigration program for graduates may not exist anymore. Then you are stuck with a degree with no advantage. Remember you are also sinking at least 4 years plus time to work (?) and lots of money to get a US degree.

If you wanted to study abroad for the reason of study, again it's all good. But if you are studying abroad just for immigration, you might be chasing phantom. Who knows what happens next year even, let alone 4-6 years from now. Trump may get another term, or not, or he isn't the president long before the next election cycle. Nobody has the crystal ball.

Little bit of story: After I finished the degree and started working in Canada, and was waiting to apply PR, they changed the system 2 months before I became eligible and qualify to apply. And I was stuck. I had to find another stream that I am eligible for and delayed over a year to apply.

Some trade skills are more desirable like plumbing, electrician, (maybe) mechanics, etc. also some human-facing jobs like teachers, daycare are less prone to be replaced by bots. Just a few ideas. The more dirty and dangerous the work, the more desirable it is.

The uncertainty of immigration policy makes me weary about using education as path to immigration. You may want to go to the UK for a visit before making the decision on whether to move to the UK for good.

You need to renew your British Citizen passport using the link below if you don't have a current British passport -

https://www.gov.uk/overseas-passports

Before you move to the UK, you may want to open an UK bank account in Hong Kong. HSBC offers this service, please see link below -

https://www.hsbc.com.hk/personal/hsbc-premier/features/premier-overseas-account.html

If you got a Hong Kong driving licence by passing a driving test in Hong Kong, you can exchange your Hong Kong driving licence to an UK one without test once you arrived in the UK.

Once you have your British Citizen passport, you can move to the UK at any time you wanted. If you have a wife / husband / child who is not a British or European Union citizen, you will need to get the correct visas for them before moving to the UK.

As a British Citizen coming back to the UK to settle, you are entitled to the National Health Service (NHS) as soon as you arrived. However, you cannot get any social welfare benefit until you lived in the UK for at lease three months.

You should go and get a National Insurance (NI) number as soon as you are in the UK. Please see link below for details -

https://www.gov.uk/apply-national-insurance-number
